title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about Salvation
confidence_notes: Doctrine of Salvation sermon on John 3:16-21. Walks through four questions (why saved, who saves, how know, what saved for): we are perishing under God's wrath; the triune God saves — Father sending, Son descending and being lifted up on the cross, Spirit giving new birth; received by grace alone through faith alone; and salvation is past (justified), present (eternal life as living union with Christ), and future (glorification). Topics chosen to capture the trinitarian shape of salvation, the finished work of Christ, the instrumentality of faith, and the present assurance believers have.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Church Part I
confidence_notes: Doctrine of the Church Part I focuses on the identity of the church from Acts 1-2 and the word 'they' in Acts 2:42. Four images: community (created for and reconciled into), family (Jesus defines family by doing the Father's will — the most common NT metaphor), temple (God's Spirit dwells in his people corporately), and kingdom outpost (a community that confesses and lives under King Jesus). Closes with baptism as the outward sign of inclusion. Topics chosen to surface the church's identity, communal life, Spirit's presence, the ordinance of baptism, and the church's worship and witness.

title: Firm Foundation: What We Believe about the Church Part II
confidence_notes: Doctrine of the Church Part II focuses on the practices of the church from Acts 2:42 — the ordinary means of grace: the apostles' teaching (Scripture as the church's authority), fellowship (interdependent care and shared life), the breaking of bread (the Lord's Supper), and prayers. Treats baptism as the doorway into the church and the Lord's Supper as the family table the Lord has given. Emphasizes the Spirit's work in producing these practices, the devotion of the early believers, and the common witness that flows outward as the Lord adds to their number day by day.

title: The Golden Calf
confidence_notes: Exodus 32 sermon on Israel's idolatry with the golden calf. Defines idolatry as trusting created things rather than the Creator for hope, happiness, significance, and security, and shows it is cultivated over time (Israel fell back to surrounding cultural patterns). Then turns to God's response — Moses's two petitions reveal God as both merciful (relenting from judgment because of his name and his covenant) and just (the Levites' sword, Moses denied as mediator). Where do we see Jesus? Moses is an imperfect intercessor whose life cannot atone; only Christ, the spotless Lamb, drinks the cup of God's wrath in our place and preserves both God's mercy and his justice. Topics chosen to surface the sin of idolatry (tagged temptation-and-sin), the character of God, the atoning work of Christ, the law/gospel contrast, God's sovereign mercy, and the covenant context.

title: The Holiness of Hunkerin'
confidence_notes: Psalm 11 sermon on holy 'hunkering down' — taking refuge in the Lord when the foundations are shaking. Three reasons for holy hunkering: (1) the Lord is in his holy temple, his throne in heaven — he hasn't changed address, so he is the bedrock stability for shifting cultural sands; (2) God uses troubles to test (his eyes see, his eyelids scrutinize), refining the righteous like gold in a craftsman's fire and growing them in steadfastness; (3) God promises something better — the upright shall behold his face, a future of righteousness in the new creation that makes every trial worth it. Closes at the Lord's table, framing communion as both looking back to the cross and forward to the day we see him face to face. Topics chosen to capture the God's unchanging character, the refining purpose of trials, the covenant hope, the present call to repentance and faith, the sanctifying work of the Spirit, and the worship context of the Lord's Supper.
